title: mentha × verticillata var. fallax
intro: Mentha × verticillata var. fallax, also known as Corn Mint or Bowles' Mint, is a hybrid species of mint plant, resulting from the cross between Mentha canadensis and Mentha aquatica. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and digestive properties, which have been found to aid in the relief of digestive issues, headaches, and skin irritations. The health benefits of consuming or applying Mentha × verticillata var. fallax include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and providing relief from nausea and respiratory issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its essential oil can be extracted for use in aromatherapy and topical applications.
